seoTitle: How to Bulk Add Classes to Transactions in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to bulk add classes to transactions in QuickBooks Online so you can update many entries at once instead of editing each individually.
intro: Manually adding a class to dozens of transactions one at a time wastes time you don't have. This guide covers how to bulk assign classes to transactions in QuickBooks Online so you can update many records in one pass.

steps.heading: Open the transaction list
steps.body: Go to the list of transactions, such as the bank feed or a filtered register, that need class assignments.

steps.heading: Select multiple transactions
steps.body: Use the checkboxes to select the batch of transactions you want to update together.

steps.heading: Apply the class in bulk
steps.body: Choose the batch edit option and assign the correct class to all selected transactions at once.

steps.heading: Confirm the changes
steps.body: Review the transactions afterward to make sure the class was applied correctly across the batch.

faq.question: What is a class used for in QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Classes let you tag transactions to track performance across departments, locations, or other business segments.

faq.question: Is bulk class assignment available on all plans?
faq.answer: Class tracking and batch actions are typically available on higher-tier QuickBooks Online plans.

faq.question: Can I undo a bulk class assignment?
faq.answer: You can go back and edit transactions individually or in another batch if the wrong class was applied.

faq.question: Does bulk adding classes work for both income and expenses?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply classes in bulk to both income and expense transactions as needed.
